title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: How to Make Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani — Easy Indian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani. This easy Indian recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani recipe
authentic Indian Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani — a beloved Indian classic

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a classic Indian dish featuring tender chicken simmered in a rich, creamy tomato-based gravy. This beloved curry is known for its mild spice, buttery texture, and a hint of sweetness, making it a favorite for all ages. It's truly a comforting and flavorful experience that embodies the essence of Indian cuisine.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1/2 tsp red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and salt to taste. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook until browned on all sides and mostly cooked through, about 8-10 minutes. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add another 1 tbsp butter. Once melted, add the chopped onion and cook until soft and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and green chilies (if using), and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, remaining 1/2 tsp red chili powder, cumin powder, and coriander powder. Cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes soften and the mixture thickens slightly.

instructions.title: Blend the Gravy
instructions.text: Remove the pan from heat and let the mixture cool slightly. Carefully transfer the gravy base to a blender and blend until completely smooth. If needed, add a splash of water to achieve a smooth consistency. Strain the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan for an extra smooth texture, pressing down on solids.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: Return the pan with the smooth gravy to medium-low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces to the gravy. Stir in the remaining 2 tbsp butter, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and sugar. Simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the gravy. If the gravy is too thick, add a little water.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the heavy cream and crushed kasoori methi. Cook for another 1-2 minutes, but do not boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust salt and sugar if necessary. Garnish with fresh chopped coriander leaves and a drizzle of extra cream. Serve hot with naan, roti, or basmati rice.

tips: For an exceptionally smooth gravy, ensure you blend the tomato-cashew mixture thoroughly and then pass it through a fine-mesh sieve. This removes any small bits and gives that restaurant-quality texture.
Marinating the chicken for at least 2-4 hours, or even overnight, significantly enhances its flavor and tenderness. Do not skip this step for the best results.
Always add the heavy cream and kasoori methi at the very end and avoid boiling the gravy afterwards, as this can cause the cream to split. Gently simmer for just a minute or two to incorporate.
article:
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, a dish synonymous with the rich tapestry of Indian cuisine, is more than just a meal; it's an experience. Its creamy texture, smoky aroma, and perfectly balanced sweet and tangy notes have captivated palates worldwide, cementing its status as a beloved Indian lunch recipe. If you've ever savored its comforting embrace at your favorite Indian restaurant and wished you could recreate that magic at home, you're in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through crafting the ultimate Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, transforming your kitchen into a hub of aromatic delight. Get ready to impress your family and friends with an authentic taste of India that’s surprisingly achievable.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

At its heart, Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a classic Indian curry featuring succulent pieces of chicken enveloped in a velvety, rich tomato-based gravy. The name itself, "Murgh Makhani," translates to "chicken with butter," hinting at the generous use of butter and cream that gives the dish its characteristic luxuriousness. Originating in the kitchens of Moti Mahal in Delhi in the 1950s, it was conceived as a way to utilize leftover tandoori chicken by simmering it in a fresh tomato gravy enriched with butter and cream.

What makes Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani so utterly irresistible is its harmonious blend of flavors. The sweetness from tomatoes and a touch of sugar is beautifully offset by the tang of yogurt and lemon, while a melange of aromatic spices – including garam masala, fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi), and cardamom – adds depth and warmth. The chicken, often marinated in yogurt and spices, is traditionally cooked in a tandoor (clay oven) or pan-fried until tender, then introduced to the luscious gravy, absorbing all its exquisite flavors. It’s a dish that truly embodies comfort and indulgence, making it a perfect centerpiece for any Indian lunch.

## Ingredients You Need

To embark on your Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani journey, gather these fresh and aromatic ingredients:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain full-fat yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri for color)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice
*   1/2 teaspoon salt

For the Gravy Base:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, roughly chopped
*   2-3 ripe large tomatoes, roughly chopped
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes, optional but recommended for smoothness)
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1-2 green chilies, slit (adjust to taste)
*   1/2 cup water

For the Main Gravy:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   2-3 green cardamom pods
*   2 cloves
*   1 small cinnamon stick
*   1 bay leaf
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri)
*   1 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1/2 teaspoon cumin powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 teaspoon sugar (or to taste)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt (or to taste)
*   1/2 cup heavy cream
*   1 teaspoon kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   Fresh coriander leaves for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create a show-stopping Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well to ensure every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours for maximum flavor and tenderness.

2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat a tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until the chicken is lightly charred on the outside and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

3.  **Prepare the Gravy Base:** In the same pan, add 2 tablespoons of butter and 1 tablespoon of oil. Once melted, add the chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, cooking for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the chopped tomatoes and soaked cashews. Add 1/2 cup of water, bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes until the tomatoes are very soft.

4.  **Blend the Gravy Base:** Let the mixture cool slightly, then transfer it to a blender. Blend until incredibly smooth. For an extra velvety texture, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing down to extract all the liquid and discarding any solids. This step is key for that restaurant-quality smooth Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani.

5.  **Assemble the Gravy:** In a clean pot or the same pan, melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the whole spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon stick, bay leaf) and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant. Pour in the sieved tomato-cashew paste. Cook, stirring frequently, for 5-7 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken and the butter to separate slightly.

6.  **Add Spices and Chicken:** Stir in the red chili powder, ground coriander, cumin powder, garam masala, sugar, and salt. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly. Add the cooked chicken pieces to the gravy. Mix well and simmer for 5-10 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the flavors of the sauce.

7.  **Finish with Cream and Kasuri Methi:** Reduce the heat to low. Stir in the heavy cream and the crushed kasuri methi. Simmer for just 2-3 minutes more, ensuring the cream doesn't curdle. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Remove from heat.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-style Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is all about attention to detail.

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't rush the marination process. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight in the refrigerator is ideal.
*   **Sear for Flavor:** Cooking the chicken until it has a slight char before adding it to the gravy adds a layer of smoky depth that mimics traditional tandoor cooking.
*   **Smooth Gravy:** Blending the gravy base thoroughly and then straining it through a fine-mesh sieve is the secret to a silky-smooth, lump-free curry. Do not skip this step if you desire that luxurious texture.
*   **Quality Ingredients:** Use good quality, ripe tomatoes, fresh ginger-garlic paste, and full-fat dairy for the richest flavor and texture. Kashmiri red chili powder is recommended for its vibrant color without excessive heat.
*   **Don't Skimp on Kasuri Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves are a signature flavor in Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani. Crush them between your palms before adding to release their aromatic oils.
*   **Balance of Flavors:** Taste and adjust the sugar, salt, and lemon juice until you achieve a perfect balance of sweet, tangy, and savory.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments. For a classic Indian lunch, serve it hot with fluffy basmati rice, fragrant jeera rice, or warm, pillowy naan bread. It also goes exceptionally well with roti, paratha, or even a simple crusty bread to scoop up every last drop of that incredible gravy. Garnish generously with fresh coriander leaves and a swirl of extra cream before serving for an inviting presentation. A side of thinly sliced red onions or a simple cucumber salad can provide a refreshing contrast.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani recipe is a masterpiece, there are several ways to adapt it to suit different preferences or dietary needs:

*   **Healthier Version:** To reduce the fat content, use low-fat yogurt for marination, reduce the amount of butter, and use light cream or even a splash of milk instead of heavy cream.
*   **Vegetarian Option:** Swap the chicken for paneer (Indian cottage cheese) to make Paneer Makhani, or use firm tofu or cauliflower florets for a delicious plant-based alternative.
*   **Vegan Option:** For a completely vegan version, use a plant-based yogurt for marination, replace butter with a neutral oil or vegan butter, and use full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream instead of dairy cream.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder and green chilies to make it as mild or spicy as you prefer.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For an authentic smoky flavor reminiscent of tandoor cooking, once the gravy is ready, place a small, heat-proof bowl in the center. Heat a small piece of charcoal until red hot, place it in the bowl, drizzle a teaspoon of ghee or oil over it, and immediately cover the pot for 5-10 minutes to infuse the smoke.

In conclusion, Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a testament to the richness and depth of Indian culinary traditions. By following this detailed guide, you can recreate this iconic dish in your own kitchen, bringing the vibrant flavors of India to your dining table. Whether it's for a special occasion or a comforting weekday meal, this flavorful and creamy curry is guaranteed to be a hit. So, roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and prepare to savor the delightful experience of homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani!
